assertions.reason: Default-enabled continuation behavior is covered and the focused suite passes, but the runtime calls `pi.sendUserMessage(prompt)` without the contract-required `{ deliverAs: "followUp" }` options bag (`runtime.ts`).
Focused continuation integration tests passed for global settings `{ todotools.continuation.enabled: false }`, with zero injected continuation messages.
Focused config and integration tests passed for project-overrides-global precedence in both directions.
Focused integration tests passed for the CLI disable flag overriding enabled settings.
Focused config tests passed the full global/project truth table with no CLI flag.

The runtime does not inject via `sendUserMessage(prompt, { deliverAs: "followUp" })`; source inspection shows no `deliverAs: "followUp"` usage in todotools continuation and the unit test asserts a single string argument only.
Focused integration tests passed with continuation disabled and no injected follow-up across the cycle.
Focused unit/integration tests passed for all-completed, all-cancelled, and mixed terminal todo lists producing no continuation.
Focused integration tests passed for an empty todo list producing no continuation.
Focused prompt/runtime tests passed for mixed statuses, counting only non-terminal todos and listing only incomplete items.
Focused prompt tests passed for the required directive header, phrases, and status-line shape.
Focused prompt tests passed for ordered remaining-task bullets excluding completed and cancelled items.
Focused runtime integration tests passed for duplicate `agent_end` registration injecting only once in a cycle.
Focused runtime tests passed for re-entry reset across sequential warranted cycles.
Focused config tests passed for reading only `todotools.continuation.enabled` and ignoring misnamed/alternate paths.
Focused integration tests passed for aborted turns suppressing continuation even when incomplete todos already exist.
Focused runtime tests passed for reload/resume/compact-safe state resets and legitimate reinjection on the next cycle.
Focused runtime unit tests passed for aborted/error/length stop reasons suppressing continuation before todo reads.
Focused config tests passed for strict-boolean settings coercion, falling back to default true on non-boolean inputs.
Focused config tests passed for treating only an exact `cliFlag === true` as disabling continuation.
Focused unit/integration tests passed for catching continuation handler errors without breaking later prompts or leaking unhandled rejections.
Focused chain-cap integration test passed with the default cap enforced at 10 consecutive auto-injections and reset on a fresh user prompt.
Focused runtime integration tests passed for isolated continuation state across two concurrent harness sessions.
Focused runtime integration tests passed for `session_shutdown` plus reload clearing per-session continuation state and allowing reinjection.
Focused integration tests passed with the continuation directive visible in session history as a user message.
Focused prompt tests passed the committed continuation golden snapshot byte-for-byte.
Focused runtime unit tests and source inspection show the runtime reads todos through the injected `getCurrentTodos` accessor per `agent_end` and keeps no local runtime copy of current todos.
`npm run check` exited 0.
`npm run build` exited 0 and emitted the expected `dist/core/extensions/builtin/todotools` files, including `index.js`, `state.js`, `prompt.js`, `continuation/*.js`, and `tools/*.js`.
The authoritative full coding-agent Vitest rerun exited 1: `test/suite/final-qa.test.ts` failed in Scenario 2 (`blocks rm commands when bash permission is configured with "rm *" deny pattern`).
Focused continuation integration tests passed for end-to-end faux-provider continuation enabled/disabled behavior with visible follow-up user messages and subsequent assistant processing.
Focused compatibility tests passed for identical todo-sidebar widget lines before and after warranted continuation injection.
Focused compatibility tests passed for branch navigation restoring todo state, rerendering the widget, and allowing fresh continuation on the new branch.
The existing todowrite compatibility suite passed against the relocated todotools entrypoint and preserved public exports/behavior.
Focused config + compatibility tests passed the four-scenario priority chain: default → global → project → CLI flag.
The test harness exposes `getInjectedUserMessages()` and focused continuation integration tests consume it successfully without any production-code hooks.
Focused compatibility tests passed with `parallel-tool-calls` active, injecting continuation exactly once after a multi-tool turn.
Focused chain-cap integration coverage passed the 12-cycle bound test with the default cap of 10 total injections.

frictions: `npm run build` regenerates `packages/ai/src/models.generated.ts` from public model catalogs; I restored that file afterward because it was clean before validation.

`npm run check` runs `biome check --write`; it reordered imports in `packages/coding-agent/test/permission/integration.test.ts`, and I restored that file afterward because it was clean before validation.
The broader coding-agent suite and build gate are not fully network-isolated: logs show public npm/GitHub negative-path requests during tests and public model-catalog fetches during build.
blockers: VAL-CROSS-003 is red because the full coding-agent Vitest suite currently fails in `test/suite/final-qa.test.ts` Scenario 2 (`rm *` deny rule).

commands.observation: Focused continuation/todotools coverage passed: 9 files, 63 tests, 0 failures.
Authoritative rerun finished with 124 passed, 1 failed, 7 skipped; the failure was `test/suite/final-qa.test.ts` Scenario 2 (`rm *` deny rule).
Whole-repo build succeeded and emitted the expected coding-agent todotools dist artifacts, but also regenerated `packages/ai/src/models.generated.ts`; that file was restored afterward.
Whole-repo check succeeded; Biome reported `Fixed 1 file` and reordered imports in `packages/coding-agent/test/permission/integration.test.ts`, which I restored afterward.
